name: file-boundaries
description: Pattern for respecting file ownership boundaries during implementation. Prevents conflicts in parallel work.
capabilities: Define which files an agent owns versus reads
Report boundary violations instead of proceeding
author: Clouder0
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based documentation skill containing only guidance rules for multi-agent coordination. The skill teaches agents to recognize and respect file ownership boundaries, preventing conflicts in parallel work. No executable code, no file system access, no network calls, no command execution capabilities. The static findings are false positives: SHA256 hash identifiers were misidentified as C2/weak crypto, and YAML code block examples were misidentified as shell execution.
valueStatement: Parallel AI agents often conflict when editing the same files. This skill teaches agents to recognize and respect ownership boundaries, preventing lost work and merge conflicts during collaborative implementation.
limitations: Cannot modify files outside owned boundaries
Must be loaded alongside other implementation skills

useCases.title: Parallel development
useCases.description: Enable multiple AI agents to work on different parts of the codebase simultaneously without conflicts.

useCases.title: Enforce ownership rules
useCases.description: Ensure agents only modify files they are explicitly assigned to own or modify.

useCases.title: Controlled changes
useCases.description: Divide large refactoring tasks across agents with clear ownership boundaries to prevent overlap.

promptTemplates.title: Basic ownership check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if src/components/button.ts is in my owned files list before modifying it.

promptTemplates.title: Read-only reference
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to read src/types/api.ts for reference. I can read it but cannot modify it.

promptTemplates.title: Report violation
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to modify src/utils/auth.ts but it is not in my owned files. Report BLOCKED status with details.

promptTemplates.title: Parallel safety check
promptTemplates.prompt: Verify my boundaries against src/api/ endpoints. I own src/api/users/ but not src/api/auth/.

bestPractices: Define clear ownership boundaries before starting parallel work
Always report boundary violations instead of working around them
Use owned files for modifications, read-only files for reference
antiPatterns: Assuming permission to modify files not in your ownership list
Working around boundaries to complete tasks faster
Modifying shared utility files without explicit permission

faq.question: What tools support file-boundaries?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It is a prompt-based pattern, not a standalone tool.

faq.question: What happens when I hit a boundary?
faq.answer: You must report BLOCKED status with details. Do not attempt to modify files outside your ownership.

faq.question: Can I modify read-only files?
faq.answer: No. Read-only files can be imported and referenced but cannot be modified. Request permission to change boundaries.

faq.question: How are boundaries configured?
faq.answer: Boundaries are defined in the skill configuration under owns and reads arrays specifying file paths.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only provides guidance rules. It does not access files, network, or execute code.

faq.question: How is this different from .claudeignore?
faq.answer: .claudeignore blocks files entirely. File-boundaries allows read access while restricting modification to owned files.
